summary: First testing iteration for e-Darpan UX Flow diagram builder. All backend endpoints (GET /api/, POST /api/status, GET /api/status, validation error) pass. All frontend features tested: 3 tabs switch correctly, node click populates side panel, auto-layout LR/TB visibly repositions nodes (verified via bounding box comparison before/after), dashed return edges rendered in red (rgb(230,57,70)), all 6 add-node buttons add nodes, reset restores original count, toggle-dashed converts selected edge to red dashed, reverse button clicked without errors (source/target attrs not exposed on DOM so swap verified via no-error execution), export-json triggers download, fullscreen button clickable.

issues: Console warning: 'It looks like you've created a new nodeTypes or edgeTypes object.' — nodeTypes IS defined at module level, but the warning still fires. Consider wrapping in useMemo or ensuring it's not recreated. Non-blocking.

action_items: Optional: silence React Flow nodeTypes warning by memoizing or verifying reference stability.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is 725 lines — consider splitting Canvas, node renderers, and toolbar into separate files for maintainability.
styleEdge() uses Math.random() in fallback id generation — can cause hydration/key mismatches if edges re-render. Prefer a deterministic counter or uuid.
deleteSelected() has stale-closure risk: it depends on `nodes` state but is used inside setEdges callback — consider passing prev nodes via functional update.
seed_data_creation: None; test-created status_checks rows prefixed TEST_ remain in DB (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is trivial (Hello World + StatusCheck CRUD). Frontend main functionality: React Flow editor with dagre layout, edge editing, tabs. All data-testids match spec. Use force=True on nodes since they're absolutely positioned. Clear localStorage before layout tests to compare original vs auto-layout positions.
